text.title: k8s-limitrange Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for k8s-limitrange as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Creating a LimitRange but omitting min values, relying only on max.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without min, containers can set requests of 1m CPU and 1Mi memory, guaranteeing scheduling on a node that cannot actually satisfy meaningful work. Min enforces a floor that makes scheduling decisions honest.

failure-mode.description: Using identical LimitRange values across production and development namespaces.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Production defaults should be higher (reflecting real workload needs and reducing BestEffort accidents); development defaults should be lower (reducing wasted cluster resources for exploratory work). Same values mean one environment is always misconfigured.

failure-mode.description: Setting default.memory too high so containers with no resources spec immediately exhaust namespace ResourceQuota.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If 20 containers each inherit a 4Gi default limit from LimitRange, and the namespace quota is limits.memory: 20Gi, a single deployment of 6 replicas exhausts the quota. LimitRange defaults must be sized against the namespace's ResourceQuota and expected pod count.
